text.heading: CONSTITUTION OF ALL SAINTS' CHAPEL UNIVERSITY OF BENIN, BENIN CITY 2009
text.p: WHEREAS we Christians belonging to the Protestant Denominations of the University of Benin and its environs are agreed that it is meet, right and a duty to worship in fellowship the Lord God Almighty through His Son, Jesus Christ, for which purpose, the All Saints' Chapel, University of Benin has been established.

AND WHEREAS we acknowledge that in such a Chapel we need to administer our affairs in an orderly, lawful and respectable manner in accordance with God's revealed will in the Holy Bible and in the spirit of tolerance, love and accommodation which is the hallmark of the Protestant tradition; we therefore, in pursuance of these ideals and of the welfare of all persons in the Chapel, on the principle of freedom, equality and justice, do hereby make, enact and give to ourselves this Constitution:
text.heading: Chapter I
text.p: GENERAL PROVISIONS
1 This Constitution may be cited as Constitution of All Saints' Chapel, University of Benin, Benin City, 2009.
2. This constitution shall come into force on January 1,2009
3.(1) In this Constitution, unless the context otherwise requires or permits, the following words or expressions shall have the following meanings:
(a) Chapel Year shall mean January to December.
(b) Convener shall mean the person who summons a meeting.

(c) Misconduct shall mean any conduct which is prejudicial to the good name or reputation of the Chapel or which undermines the proper administration of the business of the Chapel. Misconduct shall include corruption, dishonesty, stealing, adultery, embezzlement, assault, false claims against the Chapel slander, negligence of duty, suppression of information, falsification, or unauthorised destruction of accounts or records.
(d) Priest shall mean a trained and ordained Minister of God.
(e) Protestant Denomination shall be understood as all Christian communions not belonging to the Roman Catholic Church.
(f) Senior Member shall mean a member who is thirty years or more in age.
(g) University shall mean University of Benin, Benin City, Nigeria.
(2) In this Constitution, words importing the masculine gender shall include the feminine gender.
The aims and objectives of the Chapel shall be to:
(a) provide a forum for the corporate participation of Christians in divine worship in the University- of Benin;
(b) promote and entrench the ideals of practical Christian living as enunciated by our Lord Jesus Christ;
(c) foster an attitude of fervent study of the Bible and a habit of seeking, searching for and understanding the Word of God through the organisation of Christian fellowship meetings; and
(d) engage in the work of evangelism and discipleship within and outside the university.

5 The Chapel shall be named, known and addressed as All Saints' Chapel, University of Benin, Benin City, (hereinafter referred to as "the Chapel".) Branches at University of Benin Teaching Hospital (UBTH) and Ekehuan Campus are to be known as UBTH Worship Centre and Ekehuan Campus Branch respectively.

6 Membership of the Chapel shall consist of individuals who personally, willingly and by conviction accept Jesus Christ as Lord and Saviour. All such members shall have their names registered in the Chapel Register provided:
(a) they pay not less than the amount fixed as Chapel dues; or
(b) they are active in the programmes of the Chapel; or
(c) they pay their tithes regularly.
7 (1) The Chapel Services shall be open to all members of the public.
(2) All communicants of the Protestant Denomination are welcome to partake of the Holy Communion.
8 Members shall faithfully perform their spiritual, physical, moral and financial duties and obligations to the Chapel.
text.heading: Chapter II
text.p: There shall be a Chapel Congregation which shall consist of registered members.
0 The Congregation shall:
(a) be the supreme governing body of the Chapel;
(b) have powers to make policies and regulations for the Chapel;
(d) elect members of the Chapel Committee and such members of sub-committees as provided for in this Constitution except the Chaplain and the Assistant Chaplain(s); and
(e) receive and consider Annual Reports of the Chairman, Secretary, Treasurer and Auditor.
1 There shall be three types of meetings:

(a) A general meeting to be known as the Annual Meeting of Congregation (AMC) which shall be held once in every 12 (twelve) months normally in the month of February but not exceeding six weeks thereafter. The AMC shall:
(i) receive and consider annual reports of the Chairman, Secretary, Treasurer and the External Auditor; and
(ii) elect members of the Chapel Committee except the Chaplain and Assistant Chaplain(s).

(b) Emergency meeting of Congregation which may be summoned by the Chairman as the need arises, at his discretion or by a notice signed by at least 100 members of Congregation served on the Chairman and a copy pasted on the Chapel Notice Board.

(c) An Extraordinary Meeting of Congregation (EMC) shall be summoned by the Chairman of the Caretaker Committee for the purpose of electing a Chapel Committee in the event of its premature dissolution.
12(1) The date, time and agenda of any meeting shall be determined by the convener(s) of the meeting.
(2) All meetings of Congregation shall be held on Sundays within the Chapel premises.
13(1) Notice of the Annual Meeting of Congregation shall be given not less than three consecutive Sundays before the meeting is held.
(2) Notice of an Emergency Meeting of Congregation shall be at the discretion of the convener(s).
(3) Notice of an Extraordinary Meeting of Congregation shall be given not less than two Sundays before the meeting is held.
14 (1) No business shall be transacted at any meeting unless a quorum is formed. Quorum at a Congregation Meeting shall consist of 10% of, or 100 registered members, whichever is less.
(2) No business shall be transacted at any Emergency Meeting of Congregation save such as shall have been notified in the notice of the meeting.
(3) The Chairman shall preside at every meeting of Congregation except at an Extraordinary meeting. In his absence, the Deputy Chairman shall preside.
(4) Before a motion duly moved and seconded becomes a resolution of the Congregation, it must have received the support of a simple majority of those present. Voting shall be by a show of hands.
(5) In the case of equality of votes, the Chairman of the meeting at which the show of hands takes place shall be entitled to a second or casting vote.
(6) The right to vote and be voted for shall be limited to members only.
text.heading: Chapter III
text.p: CHAPEL COMMITTEE
15 There shall be a body known as the Chapel Committee.
16 The Chapel Committee shall consist of the following:
(a) Chairman, who shall be the Chaplain
(b) Deputy Chairman, who shall be a lay person
(c) Assistant Chaplain(s)
(d) Priest-in-charge, Ekenhuan Campus Branch
(e) All serving priests
(f) Chapel Secretary
(g) Assistant Secretary
(h) Treasurer
(i) Financial Secretary
(j) Assistant Financial Secretary
(k) 2 Assistant Financial Secretaries (students: male and female)
(l) Publicity Secretary
(m) People's Warden
(n) Legal Adviser
(o) Choirmaster
(p) Sunday Bible Study Superintendent
(q) Superintendent of Children's Department
(r) Internal Auditor
(s) Building Sub-Committee Chairman
(t) Harvest Sub-Committee Chairman
(u) 4 members elected from Congregation (2 of whom shall be students)
(v) 2 members elected from Ekehuan Campus congregation
(w) Youths Presidents (Ugbowo and Ekenhuan Campuses)
(x) Immediate past Deputy Chairman and Secretary.
17 Subject to the provisions of Section 10 of this Constitution, the Chapel Committee shall be responsible for the following:
(a) making of rules for the day to day running of the affairs of the Chapel;
(b) collection of revenue, expenditure of money for and on behalf of the Chapel;
(c) over-all control and superintendence of Chapel properties - movable and immovable;
(d) appointment, welfare and discipline of Chapel employees;
(e) co-ordination of the duties and responsibilities of all the sub-committees of the Chapel;
(f) preparation and recommendation of annual budget to Congregation; and
(g) appointment of members of the sub committees of the Chapel, unless otherwise stated in this Constitution.
18 The Chapel Committee shall meet once every month in addition to any other meeting that may be necessary.
19 Quorum shall be one third of the attendance).

20 With the exception of the Chaplain, Assistant Chaplain and Member Priests, members of the Chapel Committee shall be elected by Congregation at the Annual Meeting of Congregation (AMC) and shall hold office for a period of two years, (except the Chairman of the Harvest Subcommittee who shall hold office for one year) but shall thereafter not be eligible for further election to the same office until two years have elapsed following the end of the single term.
21 The Chairman shall preside at all meetings of the Chapel Committee and the Budget Sub-committee.
22 The Deputy Chairman shall assist the Chairman as appropriate, and act in his place whenever the Chairman is absent or otherwise unable to perform his duties.
23 The Chaplain shall:
(a) give spiritual leadership in the Chapel;
(b) be responsible for the arrangement of divine services in the Chapel and for other uses to which the Chapel may be put;
(c) be responsible for the day to day administration of the Chapel; and
(d) co-ordinate the activities of priests who are members of or associated with the Chapel.

24 The Assistant Chaplain(s) shall assist the Chaplain in the performance of his duties and act for him in his absence except in his capacity as Chairman of Congregation, the Chapel Committee and the Budget Sub-Committee.
25 The Chapel Secretary shall;
(a) serve as Secretary of Congregation, Chapel Committee, Budget Sub- Committe and any other Committee as the Congregation or Chapel Committee may direct; and
(b) summon meetings as directed by the Chairman and take minutes of such meetings.
The Assistant Chapel Secretary shall assist the Chapel Secretary in the performance of his duties and serve as Secretary in his absence.
27 The Treasurer shall:
(a) be responsible for keeping the financial accounts of the Chapel; and
(b) receive all monies accruing to the Chapel from the Financial Secretary and bank same within two (2) days from the date of receipt.
28 The Financial Secretary shall be responsible for:
(a) the receipt of all monies collected at Chapel services and other occasions, and for all dues, levies etc.; and
(b) keeping proper records of all monies collected and shall hand over such monies to the Treasurer not later than 2 days from the date of receipt.
29 The Assistant Financial Secretary shall assist the Financial Secretary in his duties.
30 The Assistant Financial Secretary (Student) shall assist the Financial Secretary particularly in collecting dues, levies, etc. from the students.
31 The Publicity Secretary shall publicize the activities of the Chapel.
32 The People's Warden shall:
(a) represent the congregation in matters affecting the Chapel as the occasion arises;
(b) supervise the Ushers in their functions; and
(c) present the offerings to the officiating priest for blessing.
33 The Legal Adviser shall give legal advice
34 The Choirmaster shall:
(a) be responsible for organizing and maintaining an active choir; and
(b) ensure that all the equipment of the Choir is safe and in good working condition always.
35 Sunday Bible Study Superintendent shall be responsible for the coordination and overall superintendence of Sunday Bible study.
36 Superintendent of Children's Department shall be responsible for the co-ordination and overall superintendence of Children's Department.
37 Serving Priest(s) shall perform any duty or duties that may be assigned to them when the need arises by the Chaplain, Chapel Committee or Congregation.
